description: TLA+ Formal Specifications for BurnBuddy API — model request/response protocols, concurrent operations, Firestore state transitions, and background processes to verify safety invariants

userStories.title: TLA+ project structure, Common module, and README
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TLA+ specs organized in a clear directory structure with a shared types module and documentation so I can develop and run specs locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/ directory with subdirectories: friends/, burn-buddies/, burn-squads/, workouts/, users/, notifications/
Create specs/tla/Common.tla shared module defining: Uid, Timestamp, Status, WorkoutType, WorkoutStatus types using TLA+ CONSTANTS and type-invariant operators
Create specs/tla/README.md explaining directory structure, how to install TLA+ Toolbox, how to run TLC CLI (java -cp tla2tools.jar tlc2.TLC SpecName.tla), and how to interpret TLC output
README includes link to TLA+ learning resources
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Friend Management TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of friend request flows so I can verify no duplicate requests or invalid state transitions occur under concurrency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/friends/FriendManagement.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model states: no relationship, pending request, friends
Model operations: send request, accept request, delete friend
Model concurrent scenarios: two users sending requests simultaneously, accept during delete

Define invariants: at most one pending request per direction between two users, friend relationship is symmetric (bidirectional), cannot send friend request to self, cannot accept request user didn't receive
Create specs/tla/friends/FriendManagement.cfg TLC config with 2-3 users and appropriate bounds
TLC verifies all invariants with no violations (run java -cp tla2tools.jar tlc2.TLC and confirm 0 errors)
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Burn Buddy Management TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of burn buddy flows so I can verify friend-before-buddy enforcement and request lifecycle correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/burn-buddies/BurnBuddyManagement.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model states: no buddy, pending request, active buddy
Model operations: send buddy request, accept request, update schedule, delete buddy
Model dependency on friend relationship (friendship required before buddy request)

Define invariants: cannot create burn buddy request without existing friendship, at most one burn buddy relationship per user pair, burn buddy UIDs are sorted (uid1 < uid2), at most one pending request per direction, cannot accept request without being recipient
Create specs/tla/burn-buddies/BurnBuddyManagement.cfg TLC config with 2-3 users
TLC verifies all invariants with no violations
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Burn Squad Management TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of squad operations so I can verify admin permissions, member management, and join request correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/burn-squads/BurnSquadManagement.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model states: squad with admin, members, pending join requests
Model operations: create squad, invite member, accept invite, update settings, delete squad
Model admin vs member permissions and onlyAdminsCanAddMembers setting enforcement

Define invariants: squad always has exactly one admin, admin is always in memberUids, cannot invite non-friends, only recipient can accept join request, if onlyAdminsCanAddMembers=true only admin can send invites, only admin can update settings or delete squad
Create specs/tla/burn-squads/BurnSquadManagement.cfg TLC config with 3 users
TLC verifies all invariants with no violations
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workout Lifecycle and Group Detection TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of workout start/end and group workout detection so I can verify deduplication, time windows, and concurrent workout scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/workouts/WorkoutLifecycle.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model workout states: active, completed; model operations: start workout, end workout, auto-end stale workouts (90-min threshold)
Model concurrent background process for group workout detection with abstract time states (BeforeWindow, WithinWindow, AfterWindow)
Model 20-minute detection window for buddy and squad group workouts

Define invariants: user can only end their own workouts, completed workouts have endedAt, at most one GroupWorkout per buddy/squad per 20-minute window (deduplication), GroupWorkout for buddy requires both members active within window, GroupWorkout for squad requires ALL members active within window, detection doesn't block workout creation
Create specs/tla/workouts/WorkoutLifecycle.cfg TLC config with 2-3 users and max 3-5 workouts per user
TLC verifies all invariants with concurrent workout starts (model check passes)
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Most complex spec — uses TLA+ processes for background tasks, abstract time states to avoid state space explosion. Use symmetry sets for users where possible.

userStories.title: User Profile Management TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of profile operations so I can verify username uniqueness, profile picture lifecycle, and atomic username changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/users/UserProfileManagement.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model profile states: nonexistent, created, username reserved
Model operations: create profile, update profile, change username, upload/delete profile picture
Model username reservation mechanism (usernames collection) with bounded retry logic (max 3 attempts for collisions)

Define invariants: each username (case-insensitive) is unique across all users, username changes are atomic (old reservation deleted + new created together), profile creation is idempotent (409 if already exists), cannot have profile without username reservation, cannot have orphaned username reservations
Create specs/tla/users/UserProfileManagement.cfg TLC config with 2-3 users
TLC verifies all invariants with no violations
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Push Notifications TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formal spec of notification flows so I can verify partner/squad member targeting and fire-and-forget error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/notifications/PushNotifications.tla that EXTENDS Common
Model notification trigger: workout started by user
Model concurrent background process for notification sending (async, fire-and-forget)
Model recipient collection: burn buddy partners + squad members (excluding self)

Define invariants: notification failures do not block workout creation response, user never receives notification for their own workout, all burn buddy partners and squad members are targeted, users without FCM tokens are skipped (no error)
Create specs/tla/notifications/PushNotifications.cfg TLC config with 2-3 users
TLC verifies all invariants with no violations
Add inline comments explaining the model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cross-Domain Invariants TLA+ spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a spec that verifies invariants spanning multiple domains so I can catch relationship dependency violations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/CrossDomainInvariants.tla that imports all domain specs (Friends, BurnBuddies, BurnSquads, Workouts, Users)

Define cross-domain invariants: deleting friend prevents new burn buddy request, deleting burn buddy orphans associated GroupWorkouts (by design — document), deleting burn squad orphans associated GroupWorkouts (by design — document), GroupWorkout references valid buddy/squad (referenceId exists), GroupWorkout.memberUids matches referenced buddy/squad membership at creation time, profile must exist before user can create friends/buddies/squads/workouts
Create specs/tla/CrossDomainInvariants.cfg TLC config
TLC verifies all cross-domain invariants with no violations
Document any discrepancies found as inline comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on all domain specs (US-002 through US-007) being complete.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions TLC model-checking workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TLC model checker integrated into CI so specs are automatically verified on every PR.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .github/workflows/tla-model-check.yml
Workflow triggers on PR changes to specs/tla/**/*.tla and services/api/src/**/*.ts
Workflow installs Java and downloads tla2tools.jar
Workflow runs TLC on all .tla files in parallel (one job per spec)
Each spec has 10-minute timeout
Workflow fails PR if any invariant violations found
Workflow posts comment on PR with TLC output if violations occur
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: API verification report mapping specs to implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a documented comparison of TLA+ specs against actual route handler code so I can identify implementation gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Create specs/tla/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md
For each domain spec, create a checklist mapping invariants to code locations in services/api/src/routes/*.ts
For each invariant, document: code file and function, status (✅ correctly implemented, ⚠️ partially implemented, ❌ not enforced), and any discrepancies
Summary section listing all gaps requiring fixes with severity (critical/medium/low)
Reference specific line numbers in route handlers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on all TLA+ specs and CI workflow being complete.

userStories.title: Automated tests for gaps found in verification report
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated tests covering any gaps found during spec verification so future regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: For each gap identified in VERIFICATION_REPORT.md with status ⚠️ or ❌, create or update test in services/api/src/routes/*.test.ts
Each test verifies the invariant holds (or documents why it doesn't) with a comment referencing the TLA+ spec and invariant name
All new/updated tests pass: cd services/api && yarn test
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-010 verification report identifying the gaps.
